html:
[427]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 二鬟捺林跪其前曰 - Two maidservants named Na Lin knelt before her and said.
  • 觅得薄情郎来矣 - We have found the unfeeling husband for you!
  • 予知过矣 - I know I have made a mistake.
  • 顾卿独不念斋中缱绻之情耶 - But do you not remember the tender feelings we shared in the chamber?
  • 女哂曰 - The woman laughed and said.
  • 子亦太强记 - You are too good at remembering.
  • 尚能忆及曩昔 - You can still remember the past.
  • 若奴则尽矣 - As for me, I have forgotten it all.
  • 子负心太盛 - You have been too unfaithful.
  • 即王魁、李益，有不逮焉 - Even Wang Que and Li Yi could not compare to you.
  • 尤可恨者 - What is even more hateful is...
  • 子贱玉贵珉 - You treat jade as worthless and stones as precious.
  • 致儿清白之身滥为所玷 - You have tarnished my pure body with your impurities.
  • 思之痛心切骨 - Thinking about it makes me feel pain deep in my bones.
  • 衔恨非一朝一夕矣 - I have harbored resentment for a long time.
  • 今子亲身而来 - Now you have come in person.
  • 何翅固辙之鲋 - You are no different from a fish stranded in a dried-up rut.
  • 纵摇尾乞怜 - Even if you beg for mercy by wagging your tail.
  • 亦复奚益 - It will be of no use.
  • 应示蒲鞭之辱 - You should be shown the humiliation of the reed whip.
  • 以儆狂且 - To warn the reckless and foolish.
  • 然不致子于死地者 - But I will not kill you.
  • 以子有日腾骧 - Because you will one day rise to prominence.
  • 为乃祖隐德之报故也 - To repay the hidden virtue of your ancestors.
  • 乃命二鬟褫林衣 - She then ordered the two maidservants to strip Na Lin of his clothes.
  • 折柳枝鞭之数十 - She then broke a willow branch and whipped him dozens of times.
  • 更以溪沙傅其阴 - She then covered his back with sand from the stream.
  • 置诸石上 - And placed him on a stone.
  • 而后舍去 - And then left him there.
  • 乃林之被辱也 - When Na Lin was being humiliated.
  • 身如梦魇 - He felt as if he were in a nightmare.
  • 转侧由人 - He could not move on his own.
  • 次日黎明 - At dawn the next day.
  • 方可动步 - He was finally able to move.
  • 遂踉跄而返 - He then limped back.
  • 密告符生 - He then secretly told Fu Sheng.
  • 符闻之 - Fu heard about it.
  • 浃背汗流 - He was drenched in sweat.
  • 终生不过余园 - He never went beyond Yu Garden again.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 二鬟 - Refers to two maidservants, likely of lower social status.
  • 薄情郎 - A derogatory term for an unfaithful or unfeeling husband.
  • 斋中缱绻之情 - Refers to the intimate and tender feelings shared in a private chamber.
  • 蒲鞭之辱 - Refers to a form of punishment using a reed whip, which was a common form of corporal punishment in ancient China.
  • 王魁、李益 - Historical figures known for their unfaithfulness or betrayal, used here as examples of unfaithfulness.
  • 隐德之报 - Refers to the idea of repaying the hidden virtue or good deeds of one's ancestors, a concept rooted in Confucian thought.

[428]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 自此觉私处冷如垂冰，缩似僵蚕 - A metaphorical description of a physical condition, likening it to something cold and stiff, possibly indicating impotence or a medical condition.
  • 百治不举 - Means 'failed to recover after numerous treatments,' indicating a persistent medical issue.
  • 盖已病萎 - Means 'it is likely that he has already suffered from atrophy,' suggesting a serious and possibly irreversible condition.
  • 新妇失所欢 - Means 'the new wife lost her joy,' indicating a lack of marital satisfaction.
  • 不能无外遇 - Means 'could not help but seek extramarital affairs,' suggesting a response to the lack of marital fulfillment.
  • 所赖少年英发 - Means 'relying on the youth's vigor and talent,' indicating a reliance on the younger generation's capabilities.
  • 祖德不衰 - Means 'the virtue of the ancestors does not decline,' suggesting that the family's good fortune continues.
  • 得捷南宫 - Means 'achieved success in the imperial examinations,' indicating a significant academic achievement.
  • 使致清要 - Means 'thereby attaining a high and important position,' indicating a successful career advancement.
  • 以符一子为螟蛉 - Means 'to adopt a child as a son,' indicating a common practice in ancient China where a childless couple would adopt a child.
  • 李芰裳为予言 - Means 'Li Ji Shang told me,' indicating a source of the story.
  • 不甚悉 - Means 'not very familiar,' indicating a lack of detailed knowledge.
  • 后又得赖冠千详述之 - Means 'later I heard a more detailed account from Lai Guan Qian,' indicating additional information provided by another source.
  • 恩茂先曰 - Means 'En Maoxian said,' indicating a commentary or opinion.
  • 祖有德，而子孙发甲 - Means 'the ancestor had virtue, and the descendants flourished,' suggesting a positive outcome for the family.
  • 固天所以报告人 - Means 'it is indeed how the heavens reward people,' indicating a divine or natural reward.
  • 乃又斩厥祀，殊不可解 - Means 'but then the ancestral worship was cut off, which is hard to understand,' suggesting a puzzling turn of events.
  • 闲斋曰 - Means 'Xian Zhai said,' indicating another commentary or opinion.
  • 愈远愈疏 - Means 'the further away, the more distant,' suggesting a weakening of familial ties over generations.
  • 古圣人所以有承祧之义也 - Means 'the ancient sages had the principle of continuing the lineage,' indicating a traditional practice.
  • 林生绝嗣 - Means 'Lin's line ended without an heir,' indicating a failure to produce offspring.
  • 天所以报林生，非所以报其祖 - Means 'it is a reward for Lin, not for his ancestor,' suggesting a distinction between rewards for different generations.
  • 何则林祖父有发甲之子孙，而林不得为人之祖父也 - Means 'why should it be a reward for Lin when his ancestor had descendants who flourished, and Lin himself could not become an ancestor?' indicating a logical argument.
  • 天何负于吉人哉 - Means 'how could the heavens be unfair to the virtuous?' suggesting a question about divine justice.
  • 茂先大笑叫绝 - Means 'Maoxian laughed heartily and exclaimed in approval,' indicating a positive reaction to the argument.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 南宫 - Refers to the imperial examination hall, a significant institution in ancient China for selecting government officials.
  • 符一子 - Refers to the practice of adopting a child as a son, a common practice in ancient China.
  • 螟蛉 - A term used in the context of adoption, where a childless couple would adopt a child.
  • 李芰裳 - A person who provided information about the story.
  • 赖冠千 - Another person who provided a more detailed account of the story.
  • 恩茂先 - A person who provided a commentary or opinion.
  • 闲斋 - Another person who provided a commentary or opinion.
  • 承祧 - The principle of continuing the lineage, a traditional practice in ancient China.

Proper Nouns

  • 南宫 - The imperial examination hall.
  • 符一子 - The practice of adopting a child as a son.
  • 螟蛉 - A term used in the context of adoption.
  • 李芰裳 - A person who provided information about the story.
  • 赖冠千 - A person who provided a more detailed account of the story.
  • 恩茂先 - A person who provided a commentary or opinion.
  • 闲斋 - Another person who provided a commentary or opinion.
  • 承祧 - The principle of continuing the lineage.

[429]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 署中有大书房 - The office has a large study room. '署中' refers to the office or official residence, and '大书房' indicates a spacious study.
  • 花木丛杂 - Flowers and trees are densely planted. Describes a lush, overgrown garden.
  • 廨舍宽敞 - The administrative buildings are spacious. '廨舍' refers to government offices or buildings.
  • 每见鬼物 - Often sees ghostly things. Indicates frequent encounters with supernatural phenomena.
  • 且行且唤 - Walks and calls out. Describes a person walking while speaking.
  • 绕出大堂 - Walks around the main hall. '大堂' refers to the main hall or courtyard.
  • 黯然无灯光 - Dark and without any lights. Describes a dim, lightless environment.
  • 席其体态服色 - Matches her posture and attire. Suggests that the woman resembles someone familiar to Duan Da Bin.
  • 必杏花也 - Must be Xinghua (Apricot Blossom). Likely a nickname or a reference to a specific person.
  • 酒醉兴高 - Drunk and in high spirits. Describes a state of intoxication and excitement.
  • 去数武 - Walks several steps away. '武' is an ancient unit of distance.
  • 径挝书室 - Rushes to the study room. '挝' means to knock or rush.
  • 直宿者惊起环问 - The night watchmen are startled and gather around to ask. '直宿者' refers to the night watchmen.
  • 咸闻鬼哭之声 - All hear the sound of ghostly cries. '咸' means 'all' or 'everyone'.
  • 渐出院外 - Gradually moves outside the courtyard. Describes the sound moving away.
  • 无不毛戴 - All are in a state of shock. '毛戴' is a metaphor for being terrified.
  • 备述颠末 - Fully recounts the details. '颠末' means the entire course of events.
  • 白把总至 - Captain Bai arrives. '把总' is a military rank.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 河州副总戎周公 - Governor of Hezhou, Zhou Gong. Refers to a high-ranking military official named Zhou, who is the governor of Hezhou.
  • 鬼物 - Ghostly things. Refers to supernatural phenomena, which were common in Chinese folklore and literature.
  • 杏花 - Apricot Blossom. Could be a nickname or a reference to a specific person, possibly a concubine or a servant.
  • 白把总 - Captain Bai. This is a military rank, indicating a subordinate officer.

Proper Nouns

  • 刘大宾 - Liu Da Bin. This is the name of the main character.
  • 河州副总戎周公 - Governor of Hezhou, Zhou Gong. This is the name of a high-ranking official.
  • 白把总 - Captain Bai. This is the name of a military officer.
  • 杏花 - Apricot Blossom. This could be a nickname or a reference to a specific person.

[430]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 由此病癫 - Literally means 'became ill with epilepsy,' but could also imply a broader mental or emotional disturbance.
  • 日作鬼语 - Means 'daily spoke in ghostly language,' which is likely a metaphor for erratic or incoherent speech.
  • 悉不可晓 - Means 'all could not be understood,' emphasizing the incomprehensibility of his speech.
  • 周以印花制之，不效 - The exact nature of '印花' (yanhua) is unclear; it might refer to a form of punishment or restraint, but its exact meaning is ambiguous without more context.
  • 惟偻身而行，如负重状 - Means 'only bent over and walked, as if carrying a heavy load,' describing a physical manifestation of his condition.
  • 每见杏花，则悲喜追逐 - Means 'whenever he saw Apricot Flower, he would chase after her with alternating sadness and joy,' indicating a complex emotional response.
  • 杏花亦不禁凄恻，泣数行下 - Means 'Apricot Flower could not help but feel sorrowful and shed tears,' showing her emotional reaction.
  • 周公以其怪，令二人复相见 - '周公' (Zhou Gong) is a title or honorific, likely referring to a high-ranking official. '以其怪' means 'because of his strange behavior.'
  • 杏花从此失神，或罢绣独语，或停食自伤 - Means 'Apricot Flower lost her spirit, sometimes stopped sewing and spoke to herself, or stopped eating and hurt herself,' indicating her mental state.
  • 几次投缳，皆被同人所觉 - Means 'she tried to hang herself several times, but was always discovered by others,' showing her attempts at suicide.
  • 一日，周出巡所汛，左右疏防，刘遂得于深夜潜入宅门，直抵寝所，解带缢杏花之颈 - '出巡所汛' means 'on an inspection tour of his jurisdiction,' '左右疏防' means 'the guards were lax,' and '解带缢杏花之颈' means 'unbuckled his belt and strangled Apricot Flower's neck.'
  • 比家人觉而救之，气已绝矣 - Means 'by the time the family discovered and tried to save her, she was already dead.'
  • 刘病寻愈，茫不知缢杏花之事，恨悒殊甚 - Means 'Liu's illness soon healed, but he had no memory of strangling Apricot Flower and was deeply distressed.'
  • 又日为杏花父母所窘辱，亦自缢死 - Means 'he was also daily harassed and humiliated by Apricot Flower's parents, and eventually hanged himself.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 刘 - Refers to a person named Liu, likely a noble or official.
  • 杏花 - Refers to a woman named Apricot Flower, likely a concubine or servant.
  • 周公 - A high-ranking official or noble, likely a judge or governor.
  • 印花 - A term that requires further research to determine its exact meaning in this context.
  • 投缳 - A term for attempting suicide by hanging.
  • 窘辱 - Means 'harassment and humiliation,' indicating a form of social or personal abuse.

Proper Nouns

  • 刘 - A person's name, likely a noble or official.
  • 杏花 - A person's name, likely a concubine or servant.
  • 周公 - A title or honorific, likely referring to a high-ranking official.
  • 印花 - A term that requires further research to determine its exact meaning in this context.
  • 投缳 - A term for attempting suicide by hanging.
  • 窘辱 - A term for harassment and humiliation.

[431]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 淫心一動 - Literally means 'when lustful thoughts arise,' used to describe the onset of immoral desires.
  • 招如許妖孽 - Means 'to summon such calamities,' referring to the consequences of immoral actions.
  • 不得其死 - Means 'to die an unnatural death,' often associated with a dishonorable or untimely end.
  • 夙冤 - Refers to an old grudge or enmity from a previous life or time.
  • 紅衣女何敢作祟 - Literally means 'how dare the red-clad woman cause mischief,' used to question the audacity of the supernatural entity.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 蘭岩 - The name of the commentator or author of the text, likely a scholar or literary figure.
  • 劉與杏花 - Refers to two individuals, Liu and Xinghua, whose relationship or interaction is the subject of the text.
  • 紅衣女 - A supernatural entity, possibly a ghost or spirit, often associated with red clothing in Chinese folklore.

Proper Nouns

  • 蘭岩 - The name of the commentator or author.
  • 劉 - The name of one of the individuals involved in the story.
  • 杏花 - The name of the other individual involved in the story.
  • 紅衣女 - The name of the supernatural entity.

[432]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 邱固少年，每拥衾绸，不无瞑想 - Descriptive phrase about a young man named Qiu who, when wrapped in his quilt, often engages in deep thought.
  • 時際仲春，池草重青，園夜半茁 - Describes the time of late spring, with lush green grass in the pond and the garden flourishing at midnight.
  • 薄暮，于轩东独步，瞥见一女子，年可破瓜，翠裙红袖，艳莫与京 - Vivid description of a young man walking alone in the east pavilion at dusk and seeing a beautiful young woman, about 16 years old, dressed in a green skirt and red sleeves, whose beauty is unparalleled.
  • 向邱嫣然一笑，百媚俱生 - The woman smiles at him, and her beauty comes alive.
  • 邱迷惑伫视，形如木鸡 - Qiu is so captivated that he stands still, as if turned to stone.
  • 第疑为邻女，罔敢挑达 - He suspects she might be a neighbor's daughter and dares not approach her.
  • 女寻逾垝垣而逝 - The woman quickly climbs over the broken wall and disappears.
  • 今而后知人言之不足信也 - From now on, Qiu realizes that people's words cannot be trusted.
  • 子有见而佳者，皆非土著也 - What you see as good is not from the local area.
  • 譬如今科乡试榜下，其魁卷之堪诵一时者甚少，究其实，顺天籍贯者有几人哉 - For example, in the current provincial examination, there are very few top candidates whose essays are worth reading, and even fewer are from the local area.
  • 邱大笑 - Qiu laughs heartily.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 仲春 - Refers to the second month of the lunar calendar, which is late spring.
  • 破瓜 - A euphemism for 16 years old, derived from the idea that the number 16 can be split into two 8s, which look like the character for '瓜' (melon).
  • 翠裙红袖 - Describes a green skirt and red sleeves, typical of traditional Chinese attire.
  • 順天 - Refers to the capital region of Beijing, which was known as 'Shuntian' during the Ming and Qing dynasties.
  • 鄉試 - Refers to the provincial examination, a part of the imperial examination system.
  • 魁卷 - Refers to the top-ranked essay in an examination.
  • 土著 - Refers to local residents or natives.

Proper Nouns

  • 邱 - The name of the young man.
  • 庄 - The name of Qiu's companion.
  • 仲春 - A specific time of the year.
  • 順天 - A specific region.
  • 鄉試 - A specific type of examination.
  • 魁卷 - A specific type of examination result.
  • 土著 - A specific term for local residents.

[433]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 入夜 - Literally 'entering the night,' meaning 'at night' or 'as night falls.'
  • 寤寐反侧 - A classical expression meaning 'restless sleep' or 'tossing and turning in bed.'
  • 朦胧间 - Literally 'in a daze' or 'in a haze,' meaning 'in a half-awake, half-asleep state.'
  • 倏至 - Literally 'suddenly arrives,' meaning 'appears suddenly.'
  • 瞢腾如醉 - Literally 'dazed and drunk,' meaning 'in a state of confusion and intoxication.'
  • 相与交媾 - Literally 'engage in sexual intercourse,' a direct and explicit phrase.
  • 猥亵之声四彻 - Literally 'obscene sounds spread everywhere,' meaning 'the sounds of lewdness were heard all around.'
  • 侧耳听之 - Literally 'listen closely,' meaning 'to strain one's ears to hear.'
  • 精遗满席 - Literally 'seed spills all over the seat,' meaning 'ejaculates all over the place.'
  • 起尤晏 - Literally 'rises even later,' meaning 'wakes up even later.'
  • 朝食顿减 - Literally 'morning meal suddenly decreases,' meaning 'breakfast is suddenly reduced.'
  • 举动不时 - Literally 'actions are not timely,' meaning 'behavior is irregular or out of sync.'
  • 诘其夜来何事 - Literally 'question what happened last night,' meaning 'ask about what happened last night.'
  • 秘而不宣 - Literally 'keep secret and not reveal,' meaning 'keep it hidden.'
  • 假寐伺之 - Literally 'pretend to sleep and wait for it,' meaning 'pretend to be asleep and wait to see what happens.'
  • 二更后 - Literally 'after the second watch,' meaning 'after midnight.'
  • 声作枕畔 - Literally 'sounds made beside the pillow,' meaning 'noises coming from beside the pillow.'
  • 亵语入耳喁喁 - Literally 'obscene words enter the ears softly,' meaning 'lewd whispers are heard.'
  • 精又大泄 - Literally 'seed is again greatly spilled,' meaning 'ejaculates again.'
  • 日就疲惫 - Literally 'day by day becomes tired,' meaning 'becomes increasingly exhausted.'
  • 询之 - Literally 'ask about it,' meaning 'inquire about it.'
  • 吐实 - Literally 'spit out the truth,' meaning 'tell the truth.'
  • 正色曰 - Literally 'said with a serious expression,' meaning 'said sternly.'
  • 药石也 - Literally 'medicine and stone,' an idiom meaning 'advice that is both beneficial and harsh.'
  • 敢不拜嘉 - Literally 'how dare not accept the gift,' meaning 'how could I not accept the advice.'
  • 痛绝之 - Literally 'cut off severely,' meaning 'cut off completely or punish severely.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • The passage is set in a classical Chinese context, where supernatural encounters and their moral and physical consequences are common themes. The text uses idiomatic expressions and cultural references to convey the sense of unease and the supernatural elements of the story.

Proper Nouns

  • 邱 - The name of the person involved in the supernatural encounter.
  • 庄 - The name of the person who witnesses the supernatural encounter and gives advice.

[434]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 是夕 - Refers to 'that night,' indicating a specific time.
  • 女不至 - Means 'the woman did not arrive,' where '女' refers to a woman or female.
  • 邱诩于庄 - Means 'Qiu flattered Zang,' where '诩' means to flatter or praise.
  • 庄亦心喜 - Means 'Zang was also secretly pleased,' indicating a hidden emotion.
  • 适同监涪州刘生 - Refers to 'a student named Liu from Fuzhou who happened to be present,' where '适' means to happen to be, '同监' means to be present, and '涪州' is the place name Fuzhou.
  • 精歧黄术 - Means 'skilled in the Yellow Emperor's Canon of Medicine,' where '歧黄术' refers to traditional Chinese medicine.
  • 偶遇庄 - Means 'chanced upon Zang,' indicating a random encounter.
  • 惊曰 - Means 'exclaimed in surprise,' where '惊' means to be surprised or alarmed.
  • 君何病之深也 - Means 'What illness do you have that is so severe?' where '何病之深' is an archaic way of asking about the severity of an illness.
  • 诊之 - Means 'examined him,' where '诊' means to diagnose or examine.
  • 错愕良久 - Means 'was stunned for a long time,' indicating a state of shock or surprise.
  • 君年望六 - Means 'you are almost sixty years old,' where '望六' is a roundabout way of saying 'almost sixty.'
  • 那得有遗精症 - Means 'how could you have nocturnal emissions,' where '遗精症' refers to nocturnal emissions.
  • 岂其为狐鬼所蠹乎 - Means 'could it be that you are being afflicted by a fox spirit or ghost?' where '蠹' means to afflict or harm.
  • 庄神之 - Means 'Zang was convinced,' where '神之' means to be convinced or believe.
  • 即告以邱生事 - Means 'immediately told him about Qiu's situation,' where '告以' means to inform or tell.
  • 俄邱至 - Means 'soon Qiu arrived,' where '俄' means soon or shortly after.
  • 刘亦诊之 - Means 'Liu also examined him,' where '亦' means also.
  • 怃然曰 - Means 'said thoughtfully,' where '怃然' means to look thoughtful or puzzled.
  • 此狐祟 - Means 'this is a fox spirit,' where '狐祟' refers to a fox spirit causing trouble.
  • 非鬼也 - Means 'not a ghost,' where '非' means not.
  • 无药可医 - Means 'there is no medicine to cure it,' where '无药可医' is a common idiom indicating something is incurable.
  • 城南有穆萨嘛者 - Refers to 'there is a person named Mu Sama in the south of the city,' where '穆萨嘛' is a transliteration of a name.
  • 可延至 - Means 'can be invited to,' where '延' means to invite or extend.
  • 使驱之 - Means 'to drive it away,' where '驱' means to drive away or expel.
  • 庄问穆萨嘛何如人也 - Means 'Zang asked what kind of person Mu Sama is,' where '何如' means what kind or how.
  • 刘曰 - Means 'Liu said,' where '曰' means said.
  • 厢白旗 - Refers to 'a district called Xiangbaiqi,' where '厢' means district and '白旗' means white flag.
  • 蒙古人也 - Means 'is a Mongolian,' where '蒙古人' means Mongolian.
  • 为羽林军 - Means 'serves as a guard,' where '羽林军' refers to a military guard.
  • 萨嘛者 - Refers to 'a shaman,' where '萨嘛' is a transliteration of a term for shaman.
  • 译言巫觋也 - Means 'translates to shaman,' where '巫觋' means shaman.
  • 庄敬诺 - Means 'Zang respectfully agreed,' where '敬诺' means to respectfully agree.
  • 挽邱童往邀之 - Means 'took Qiu and a child to invite him,' where '挽' means to take or accompany, '邱童' means Qiu and a child, and '邀' means to invite.
  • 穆迫于官役 - Means 'Mu was pressed by official duties,' where '迫于' means to be pressed by or forced by.
  • 约三日后甫得来 - Means 'agreed to come three days later,' where '约' means to agree or promise, '三日后' means three days later, and '甫得来' means finally came.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 狐鬼所蠹 - Refers to the belief in fox spirits and ghosts causing harm, a common theme in Chinese folklore.
  • 穆萨嘛 - A transliteration of a name, likely referring to a shaman or spiritual healer.
  • 羽林军 - Refers to the imperial guard, a military unit responsible for protecting the emperor.
  • 萨嘛 - A transliteration of a term for shaman, indicating a spiritual or religious figure.
  • 巫觋 - Refers to shamans or spiritual practitioners in traditional Chinese culture.

Proper Nouns

  • 邱 - The name of a person, Qiu.

  • 漏三下 - Refers to the third strike of the ancient timekeeping device (漏), marking the passage of time in the night.
  • 责邱曰 - '责' means 'to rebuke' or 'to scold,' and '曰' introduces direct speech.
  • 君何信刘监生语 - Rhetorical question meaning 'Why do you believe what Liu Jiansheng said?'
  • 己不与焉 - '己' means 'oneself,' and '与' means 'to participate' or 'to be involved.' Together, it suggests that the speaker was not involved in the matter.
  • 忿然 - Means 'angrily' or 'in a rage,' describing the emotional state of the speaker.
  • 接吻 - Literal term for 'kissing,' used here in a more symbolic or metaphorical sense.
  • 艻艻然气出如绠 - Vivid description of gas escaping in a thin, thread-like manner, likened to a rope ('绠').
  • 茫茫无所凭 - Means 'feeling lost and without support,' describing a state of confusion or disorientation.
  • 五内欲裂 - Hyperbolic expression meaning 'the five organs feel like they are about to burst,' describing intense physical pain or distress.
  • 白身僵卧 - Means 'lying motionless and naked,' describing a state of unconsciousness or extreme vulnerability.
  • 喧救一饷始醒 - '喧救' means 'to shout for help,' and '一饷' is a unit of time meaning 'a short while.' Together, it describes a brief period of urgency before the person regains consciousness.
  • 弟之鬼，将就馁于异乡矣 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'My spirit will starve in a foreign land,' referring to the speaker's impending death or separation from their body.
  • 大恚，向空谩骂 - '大恚' means 'great anger,' and '谩骂' means 'to curse' or 'to insult.' Together, it describes the speaker's intense frustration and cursing into the air.
  • 招邱同榻 - Means 'to invite Qiu to share the same bed,' suggesting a gesture of reconciliation or companionship.

  • 医来弗药 - Refers to the practice of not giving medicine to a patient who is in a critical condition, often seen in traditional Chinese medicine.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 漏三下，術人炷香於爐，焚符於燭，教二童俯身從胯下反視幾下圭竇，問曰：‘何所見？’曰：‘開門矣。’ This long sequence must be read as a series of actions leading up to the ritual and the children's response.
  • 術人即噙水向壁上噀之，復亟問：‘何如？’童曰：‘梳頭矣。勻面矣。’ This sequence continues the ritual and the children's responses, indicating the progression of the ritual.
  • 既而曰：‘易履矣，著衣矣。’ This phrase indicates the next stages of the ritual.
  • 術人曰：‘然則可以出矣。’ This phrase marks the conclusion of the ritual.
  • 噙水三噀之，瞥見一女子立幾後，約長五尺許，衣大紅衫，拖素裙，眉目娟好，微笑作羞恥態。 This sequence describes the apparition, including her appearance and demeanor.
  • 術人嘱曰：‘太太在此，可為禮。’ This phrase instructs the children to show respect.
  • 童子力牽，女子力卻，相持良久。 This phrase describes a struggle between the children and the apparition.
  • 術人作急狀，止之曰：‘彼修仙人，性村野，且放手，我自有處。’ This phrase explains the reason for the struggle and the resolution.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 改業已久，仍復為利動，宜鬼物戲弄之也 — This sentence introduces a cause-and-effect relationship, where the person's repeated materialistic behavior invites supernatural mockery.
  • 每見世人，當痛遭窘辱時，未始不立志變計，悔心慚熾 — This clause describes a common human response to adversity, where people resolve to change their ways and feel intense regret and shame.
  • 一旦有重金以啖之者，遂致故態復萌，捨身不顧，名行墮喪，不可收拾 — This clause describes the consequences of being tempted by substantial money, leading to a recurrence of old behavior and a loss of reputation and moral integrity.
  • 其不為鬼所侮弄，幾希矣 — This clause emphasizes the rarity of avoiding supernatural mockery, reinforcing the moral lesson.
  • 吁，可不見利思義哉 — This exclamation expresses a moral critique, urging people to consider righteousness in the face of profit.
  • 趙姥為媒多年，豈於日夕來往之地，有此大族，未之前聞耶 — This clause expresses surprise and skepticism, questioning how Aunt Zhao could be unaware of a large family in a place she visits daily.
  • 乃無疑慮，徒事跋涉，想亦利令智昏耳 — This clause describes Aunt Zhao's lack of caution and the possible explanation for her behavior, suggesting that profit has clouded her judgment.

  • 一夕寒夜思酒 - Describes a cold night when the protagonist craves wine. The phrase '思酒' (craving wine) is a common expression in classical literature.
  • 褦襶 - An ancient term referring to a type of dog, often used in classical texts to denote a loyal or clever pet.
  • 戲之曰 - Means 'teased it by saying,' indicating a playful interaction.
  • 聲呦呦，似應諾然 - Describes the dog's response as a soft, affirmative sound, similar to a nod of agreement.
  • 官乃以青蚨數十並一瓶，置其頂上 - '青蚨' refers to ancient Chinese money, and '置其頂上' means 'placed on its head.'
  • 俄頃已在面前，頂上有瓶無錢矣 - Indicates that the dog returned quickly with the bottle but without the money.
  • 取之白酒滿中，大喜 - Describes the protagonist's delight upon finding the bottle full of wine.
  • 自是零星細物，无不遣之 - Means 'from then on, no small or trivial items were sent,' indicating the dog's reliability.
  • 市物之家，但失物得钱，传以为怪 - Describes how merchants found it strange that they lost items but received money.
  • 唯官心明其故，特秘而不宣 - Means 'only the official knew the reason and kept it secret.'
  • 数年，未尝须臾离 - Indicates that the dog was always present for many years.
  • 会考满，得闽中一郡，既束装，褦襶依依，似不忍舍 - Describes the dog's reluctance to leave when the official was reassigned.
  • 官亦怅悒 - Means 'the official also felt sad.'
  • 抵闽逾岁，靡日不思 - Indicates that the official missed the dog every day after arriving in Fujian.
  • 褦襶忽至，大惊喜，呼之入室，眷属惊怔 - Describes the dog's unexpected return and the family's surprise.
  • 举家怀思，后竟不复至 - Means 'the whole family missed the dog, but it never returned.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 青蚨 - Ancient Chinese currency, often used in classical texts to denote money.
  • 闽中一郡 - Refers to a region in Fujian Province, indicating the official's new posting.
  • 束装 - Means 'to pack one's belongings,' indicating preparation for departure.
  • 依依 - Describes a reluctant or affectionate departure, often used to express emotional attachment.
  • 怅悒 - A term indicating sadness or melancholy, often used to describe emotional states.

Proper Nouns

  • 青蚨 - Ancient Chinese currency.
  • 闽中一郡 - A region in Fujian Province.
  • 束装 - To pack one's belongings.
  • 依依 - Reluctant or affectionate departure.
  • 怅悒 - Sadness or melancholy.

  • 蹀蹀徐行 - Walking slowly and cautiously, describing the movement of the two figures.

  • 其父亦鑿鑿究理者 - Means 'his father was also someone who carefully investigated things.'

  • 深夜荒郊 - Means 'deep into the night in a desolate countryside.'
  • 何所不有 - Means 'anything can happen.'
  • 況汝所遇者，刑天之流亞也 - Means 'especially since what you encountered is similar to Xingtian.'

  • 今既經此異 - Means 'now that you have experienced this strange thing.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 酒肆 - Refers to a tavern or inn, a place where people gather to drink and socialize.
  • 二鼓 - Refers to the second drumbeat, which traditionally marked the beginning of the second watch of the night (around 10 PM).

Proper Nouns

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 閱數月 - After several months, indicating a passage of time.
  • 甲復晚散 - Jia returned late from a gathering.
  • 憶家中小兒出痘 - Remembering that his young child had smallpox.
  • 不可不歸 - Could not avoid returning home, emphasizing the necessity of returning.
  • 且陰計怪異之事偶或遭之，讵必常有 - Moreover, secretly calculating that strange events might occasionally occur, but they are not always present, expressing Jia's internal thoughts about the likelihood of unusual occurrences.
  • 骡导其故道仍如曩时 - The mule led the old path as before, describing the mule's familiar route.
  • 复往其处 - Returning to that place, indicating Jia's return to the same location.
  • 方回溯当日主况 - Just recalling the situation of that day, describing Jia's attempt to remember what happened.
  • 未已 - Not yet finished, indicating that Jia's recollection was still ongoing.
  • 远远灯光，随声又来，不一而足，益而三焉 - Far-off lights, accompanied by sounds, came one after another, increasing to three in number, describing the appearance of multiple lights and sounds.
  • 甲屡选怯 - Jia repeatedly chose to be timid, indicating Jia's fear and hesitation.
  • 不待骡惊，鞭入田中 - Without waiting for the mule to be startled, he lashed it into the field, describing Jia's impulsive action.
  • 此时黍稷已获 - At this time, the millet and grain had been harvested, indicating the season and the state of the fields.
  • 一望旷朗 - A vast and clear view, describing the open and clear landscape.
  • 须臾三物，鱼贯而至，形状犹昔，唯增一男 - In a moment, three beings appeared in a line, their shapes the same as before, but with an additional male, describing the sudden appearance of three figures.
  • 骡一见惊嘶 - The mule, upon seeing them, neighed in fright, describing the mule's reaction.
  • 三物截然而止，并立向甲啾啾作声，如小儿吹葱然 - The three beings stopped abruptly and stood together, making sounds like children blowing through chives, describing the eerie sounds made by the figures.
  • 甲不觉褫魄，昏坠骡下 - Jia was so frightened that he lost his soul and fainted from the mule, describing Jia's extreme fear and fainting.
  • 其父见骡之独逸以归也，知其子有变，即鸠合家人，操兵执炬，觅至所说僻径，遍索田中，良久始获，抢攘舁归，呼救半夜始苏，更述其怪，闻者罔不错愕 - His father, seeing the mule return alone, knew something was wrong with his son, and immediately gathered the family, armed with weapons and torches, to search the described secluded path. They searched the fields for a long time before finding Jia, who was carried back in a rush. He was called for help and woke up in the middle of the night, recounting the strange events. Those who heard him were all surprised and shocked, describing the father's actions and the community's reaction.
  • 其父延缁羽为禳，不复有效，越数日竟死 - His father invited monks and Taoists to perform rituals, but they were no longer effective. After several days, Jia died, describing the unsuccessful attempts to save Jia.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 小兒出痘 - Smallpox, a common and deadly disease in ancient times, often used in literature to convey themes of suffering and mortality.
  • 骡 - Mule, a common animal used for transportation in ancient China, often symbolizing endurance and reliability.
  • 黍稷 - Millet and grain, staple crops in ancient Chinese agriculture, often used in literature to describe rural settings and agricultural life.
  • 缁羽 - Monks and Taoists, religious figures who were often called upon to perform rituals and exorcisms in times of crisis.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 甲复晚散，忆家中小儿出痘，不可不归，且阴计怪异之事偶或遭之，讵必常有 - This sentence is elliptical and grammatically compact, but it can be read as: 'Jia returned late from a gathering, remembering that his young child had smallpox, and could not avoid returning home. Moreover, he secretly calculated that strange events might occasionally occur, but they are not always present.'
  • 骡导其故道仍如曩时，复往其处，方回溯当日主况，未已，远远灯光，随声又来，不一而足，益而三焉 - This sentence is also elliptical and can be read as: 'The mule led the old path as before, returning to that place. Jia was just recalling the situation of that day when far-off lights, accompanied by sounds, came one after another, increasing to three in number.'
  • 须臾三物，鱼贯而至，形状犹昔，唯增一男 - This sentence is a concise description of the sudden appearance of three figures, and can be read as: 'In a moment, three beings appeared in a line, their shapes the same as before, but with an additional male.'
  • 其父见骡之独逸以归也，知其子有变，即鸠合家人，操兵执炬，觅至所说僻径，遍索田中，良久始获，抢攘舁归，呼救半夜始苏，更述其怪，闻者罔不错愕 - This sentence is a complex sequence of actions and reactions, and can be read as: 'His father, seeing the mule return alone, knew something was wrong with his son, and immediately gathered the family, armed with weapons and torches, to search the described secluded path. They searched the fields for a long time before finding Jia, who was carried back in a rush. He was called for help and woke up in the middle of the night, recounting the strange events. Those who heard him were all surprised and shocked.'

Proper Nouns

  • 甲 - Jia, the main character of the story.
  • 其父 - His father, referring to Jia's father.
  • 缁羽 - Monks and Taoists, religious figures who perform rituals.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 闲斋曰 - Classical formula meaning 'Idle Studio says' or 'Idle Studio states,' used to introduce personal commentary or reflection.
  • 予少游湟中 - Means 'I traveled to the region of湟 when I was young,' where 湟 refers to the湟水 (Kang River) area.
  • 临青海 - Means 'approached Qinghai Lake,' indicating the proximity to the lake.
  • 水之清如潇湘，深如彭泽 - A simile comparing the clarity of the water to that of the潇湘 (Xiao and Xiang rivers) and the depth to that of彭泽 (Pengze), both famous for their scenic beauty and depth.
  • 遥望波心烟一点 - Means 'looking far into the heart of the waves, a wisp of smoke appears,' describing a distant, almost ethereal sight.
  • 番人曰 - Means 'the locals said,' indicating that the following statement is attributed to the local people.
  • 龙驹岛也 - Means 'it is the Dragon Horse Island,' referring to an island in the lake.
  • 周回约千里 - Means 'circling about a thousand li (a unit of distance),' indicating the size of the island.
  • 其大倍于洞庭 - Means 'its size is double that of Dongting Lake,' emphasizing the vastness of the island.
  • 其后游闽，登夏门，观冥海 - Means 'later, I traveled to Fujian, climbed Xia Gate, and observed the冥海 (Minghai, a mythical sea),' indicating a subsequent journey.
  • 则青海犹盆池也 - Means 'then Qinghai Lake seemed like a small pond,' using a metaphor to describe the relative size of the lake.
  • 吁！水大亦何常之有 - An interjection expressing wonder, followed by a rhetorical question meaning 'How can the size of water be constant?' suggesting the variability of nature.
  • 所见大，则所过皆小；所见小，则所过皆大 - A philosophical statement meaning 'what one sees as large makes everything else seem small; what one sees as small makes everything else seem large,' reflecting on perception and perspective.
  • 覆杯水于堂坳之上，群蚁过之，如洪水之怀山襄陵也固宜 - A metaphorical statement meaning 'pouring a cup of water into a low place in the hall, the ants passing over it would seem like a flood surrounding mountains and hills,' illustrating the relativity of size and perception.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 闲斋 - A classical name for a study or retreat, often used to denote a place of contemplation and writing.
  • 湟中 - Refers to the湟水 (Kang River) region, historically significant for its strategic location and natural beauty.
  • 青海 - Refers to Qinghai Lake, one of the largest saltwater lakes in China, known for its clear waters and scenic beauty.
  • 潇湘 - Refers to the潇 (Xiao) and湘 (Xiang) rivers, famous for their picturesque landscapes and cultural significance.
  • 彭泽 - Refers to Pengze, a region known for its deep waters and historical importance.
  • 番人 - Refers to the local people, often used to describe non-Han ethnic groups.
  • 龙驹岛 - Refers to the Dragon Horse Island, a specific island in Qinghai Lake.
  • 闽 - Refers to Fujian, a province in southeastern China known for its coastal cities and historical significance.
  • 夏门 - Refers to Xia Gate, a specific location in Fujian, possibly a historical or geographical landmark.
  • 冥海 - Refers to the冥海 (Minghai), a mythical sea often associated with the underworld in Chinese mythology.
  • 洞庭 - Refers to Dongting Lake, one of the largest freshwater lakes in China, known for its scenic beauty and cultural significance.
  • 覆杯水于堂坳之上 - A metaphorical reference to pouring a cup of water into a low place in the hall, often used to illustrate the relativity of size and perception.

  • 即见女倚栏而待 - 即见 means 'immediately see,' 女 means 'woman,' 倚栏 means 'leaning on the railing,' and 而待 means 'awaiting.' This phrase describes the woman leaning on the railing, waiting.
  • 把握极欢 - 把握 means 'hold' or 'grasp,' and 极欢 means 'extremely happy.' Together, it suggests a deep, joyful connection.

  • 从此依倚闺中，不离跬步 - 从此 means 'from this point on,' 依倚 means 'relying on,' 闺中 means 'in the inner chamber,' and 不离跬步 means 'not leaving a step.' This phrase suggests that they became inseparable, staying close to each other.
  • 忽春罗扬声户外也 - 忽 means 'suddenly,' 春罗 is a proper name, 扬声 means 'shouting,' and 户外 means 'outside the door.' This describes Spring Luo shouting from outside the door.
  • 主母归来矣 - 主母 means 'mistress' or 'mother-in-law,' and 归来矣 means 'has returned.' This phrase announces the mistress's return.
  • 男女受授不亲，促膝可乎 - 男女受授不亲 means 'men and women should not be too intimate,' and 促膝可乎 means 'can they sit close together?' This reflects a moral concern about the appropriateness of their closeness.
  • 田惶恐投地，愿甘责罚 - 田惶恐投地 means 'Tian was terrified and fell to the ground,' and 愿甘责罚 means 'willing to accept punishment.' This shows Tian's fear and submission.
  • 媪瞋目视女，女泪萦两颊，愧而不惧 - 媪瞋目视女 means 'the old woman glared at the woman,' 女泪萦两颊 means 'the woman's tears welled up in her eyes,' and 愧而不惧 means 'ashamed but not afraid.' This describes the emotional state of the woman.
  • 留亲下榻，竟成揖盗入门 - 留亲下榻 means 'inviting a relative to stay,' and 竟成揖盗入门 means 'it turned out to be like inviting a thief into the house.' This is a metaphorical expression suggesting that inviting someone in led to trouble.
  • 为是自家侄子，且似谨愿，非嬛薄者，故坦然付托，出外不疑 - 为是自家侄子 means 'because he is a family member,' 且似谨愿 means 'and seems to be diligent and sincere,' 非嬛薄者 means 'not frivolous or shallow,' and 故坦然付托，出外不疑 means 'so I entrusted him without hesitation, not doubting him.' This explains why the old woman trusted Tian.
  • 不意亲骨肉，才半月之久，何意草创便尔，禽处兽爱 - 不意亲骨肉 means 'unfortunately, even family members,' 才半月之久 means 'only for a month,' 何意草创便尔 means 'how could they have acted so hastily,' and 禽处兽爱 means 'like animals in their behavior.' This expresses the old woman's disappointment and shock.
  • 今之所谓少年老成者，尚可信哉 - 今之所谓少年老成者 means 'what we call mature young people today,' and 尚可信哉 means 'can they still be trusted?' This is a rhetorical question expressing doubt.
  • 第事已舛谬，侄之肉亦不足食 - 第事已舛谬 means 'but the matter has already gone wrong,' and 侄之肉亦不足食 means 'even my nephew's flesh is not worth eating.' This is a hyperbolic expression of frustration.
  • 今与侄约，领老身资本二千金，往山东贩货，须志如翁伯，勿为康乐 - 今与侄约 means 'now I make a deal with my nephew,' 领老身资本二千金 means 'take my capital of two thousand gold pieces,' 往山东贩货 means 'go to Shandong to trade goods,' 须志如翁伯 means 'must have the same determination as my father-in-law,' and 勿为康乐 means 'do not be lazy.' This sets the terms of the agreement.
  • 苟能获利三倍，即当以秀姑妻汝，否则无相见也 - 苟能获利三倍 means 'if you can make three times the profit,' 即当以秀姑妻汝 means 'I will marry my daughter Xiu Gu to you,' and 否则无相见也 means 'otherwise, we will never see each other again.' This outlines the conditions for the marriage.
  • 田崩角稽首，额为之坟 - 田崩角稽首 means 'Tian fell to the ground and bowed deeply,' and 额为之坟 means 'his forehead became a mound.' This describes Tian's deep bow of submission.

  • 甫入院门，即见女倚栏而待，把握极欢 - This sequence must be read as a continuous action: 'As soon as he entered the courtyard, he saw the woman leaning on the railing, waiting, and they were extremely happy.'
  • 从此依倚闺中，不离跬步 - This phrase must be read as a single unit: 'From this point on, they relied on each other in the inner chamber, never leaving a step apart.'
  • 忽春罗扬声户外也 - This phrase must be read as a single action: 'Suddenly, Spring Luo shouted from outside the door.'
  • 主母归来矣 - This phrase must be read as an announcement: 'The mistress has returned.'
  • 男女受授不亲，促膝可乎 - This phrase must be read as a question: 'Men and women should not be too intimate; can they sit close together?'
  • 田惶恐投地，愿甘责罚 - This phrase must be read as a single action: 'Tian was terrified and fell to the ground, willing to accept punishment.'
  • 媪瞋目视女，女泪萦两颊，愧而不惧 - This phrase must be read as a series of actions: 'The old woman glared at the woman, the woman's tears welled up in her eyes, and she was ashamed but not afraid.'
  • 留亲下榻，竟成揖盗入门 - This phrase must be read as a metaphor: 'Inviting a relative to stay turned out to be like inviting a thief into the house.'
  • 为是自家侄子，且似谨愿，非嬛薄者，故坦然付托，出外不疑 - This phrase must be read as an explanation: 'Because he is a family member, and seems to be diligent and sincere, not frivolous or shallow, so I entrusted him without hesitation, not doubting him.'
  • 不意亲骨肉，才半月之久，何意草创便尔，禽处兽爱 - This phrase must be read as an expression of disappointment: 'Unfortunately, even family members, only for a month, how could they have acted so hastily, like animals in their behavior.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The long sequence '井中黑暗如夜，泥深没踝，暗中摸索，无计可出，自拼必死' describes the protagonist's situation in the well, emphasizing the darkness, mud, and despair.

  • The sequence '已而有隙光透入，望如一线；即之，得一石门，力撼之，豁然开朗' describes the sudden change from a dark, confined space to a bright, open one.

  • 患遂绝 - '患' means 'trouble' or 'illness,' '遂' means 'therefore,' and '绝' means 'ended.' Describes the trouble or illness ending.

  • 予居时每见之 - '予' means 'I,' '居' means 'lived,' '时' means 'at that time,' '每' means 'often,' and '见之' means 'saw her.' Describes often seeing her at that time.
  • 吴有《逸狐歌》 - '吴' means 'Wu,' '有' means 'had,' and '《逸狐歌》' means 'the song of the wandering fox.' Describes Wu having a song about the wandering fox.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • The Lantern Festival (上元节) is celebrated on the 15th day of the first lunar month, a major traditional Chinese festival.
  • The guards (金吾) not enforcing strict curfews or restrictions (金吾不禁) allows for more freedom and celebration during festivals.
  • 'Hu lu' (呼卢) is a traditional Chinese dice game, often played during festivals and social gatherings.
  • The Chinese night is divided into five watches, and the fourth watch (四更) corresponds to the hours of 3-5 AM.
  • Xi'an Gate (西安门) is a historical gate in Beijing, part of the city's ancient walls.
  • A teahouse (茶铺) is a common social gathering place in traditional Chinese culture.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The parallel structure '一念之善，虎可为人；玩忽旋生，人而为虎' compares the transformation of a tiger into a human and vice versa, emphasizing the thin line between virtue and vice.
  • The clause '此圣狂之间，在于几希也' explains that the difference between sainthood and madness lies in the subtlest of distinctions.
  • The phrase '虚亦非寻常兽也' emphasizes that the tiger is not an ordinary beast, suggesting its extraordinary nature.
  • The complex sentence '琰慷慨豪爽，故得变为虎，若世之险邪庸碌辈，恐欲变犬而亦不可得为守夜者矣，敢望成虎耶' contrasts the transformation of a generous and bold person into a tiger with the inability of ordinary, wicked, and mediocre people to even become watchmen, let alone tigers.
  • The introduction '傻白太监白某，面白，人称傻白' provides background information about the eunuch Bai, describing him as foolish and pale.
  • The phrase '年四十余矣' indicates that Bai is in his forties.
  • The long sentence '间尝为余言，其十六岁时，值上元节，金吾不禁，灯月交辉，从其叔之西城外祖母家，与诸姊妹兄弟呼卢半夜' describes Bai's experience during the Lantern Festival, including the relaxed atmosphere and his participation in a dice game.
  • The clause '四更后，始告归' indicates that Bai returned home after the fourth watch of the night.
  • The sentence '至半途，忽忆表妹所赠升官图一纸，骰子六枚，忘未携得，欲返取之' describes Bai's realization that he forgot to take the promotion map and dice, leading him to return to retrieve them.
  • The clause '叔不耐往还，约在西安门茶铺中坐候' explains that Bai's uncle was not patient with the back-and-forth and agreed to meet at a teahouse in Xi'an Gate.
  • The final sentence '白独返外家，取得二事，更为留连一饷，然后行' describes Bai's return to his maternal home, retrieval of the items, and additional time spent lingering before continuing his journey.

Proper Nouns

  • 金吾 - The guards or officials responsible for maintaining order.
  • 西安门 - A historical gate in Beijing.
  • 茶铺 - A teahouse.
  • 傻白太监白某 - The eunuch named Bai.

  • 两颊晕生，强笑曰 - Means 'Her cheeks turned red, and she forced a smile,' indicating a mix of embarrassment and amusement.

  • 翁止之曰：“有我在，无忧孤寡。” - The old man comforted her, saying, 'With me here, there is no worry about being alone or widowed.'

  • 翁叹息再四，命易以柏棺，亲视安葬，遂与媪俱归，豢之别室 - The old man sighed repeatedly, ordered the use of a cypress coffin, personally supervised the burial, and then returned with the old woman to a separate room where they were cared for.
  • 其家只翁一子承嗣，拥资百万 - The family had only the old man's son as the heir, who possessed a million in assets.
  • 阅年，其父死 - After a year, the father died.
  • 母二十而寡，爱翁如掌珠 - The mother, twenty years old, became a widow and loved the old man as if he were her most precious possession.
  • 翁行善好施，由于天性，逾于前生，人以为善人之报云 - The old man was kind and generous, due to his nature, surpassing his previous life, and people believed it was a reward for his goodness.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sequence '既归，以白其母，母叹且泣曰' introduces the return, the act of informing the mother, and the mother's emotional response.
  • The rhetorical question '以汝父素行，又何事不屑为，神佛岂欺人哉' emphasizes the mother's belief in her husband's character and the impossibility of divine deception.
  • The clause '嗣此戒律愈严，女亦信心奉佛焉' links the increased strictness of rules to the daughter's devotion to Buddhism.
  • The sentence '其子年虽少，颇有父风，乡人称其克肖' describes the son's resemblance to his father and the villagers' recognition of this similarity.
  • The clause '屡梗母教，母甚忧之' highlights the son's resistance to his mother's teachings and the resulting concern.
  • The sentence '一日，有亲戚归自京师者，其子往候之' introduces a visit to a returning relative and sets the stage for the conversation.
  • The question '话及京师人物众矣，究竟何等人最乐' prompts the relative to discuss the happiest people in the capital.
  • The response '乐者甚伙，要皆高不可下耳' acknowledges the many happy people but emphasizes their unattainable status.
  • The statement '尔我今生断不能及' reinforces the idea that the speaker and the listener cannot match the status of the happy people.
  • The clause '唯一等人，极可歆羡，盖太监也' identifies eunuchs as the most enviable group.
  • The question '刑余之人，有何可乐' challenges the idea of happiness among eunuchs.
  • The statement '汝但知其人道已绝，必乏乐趣' reflects a common belief about the lack of human nature and pleasure among eunuchs.
  • The clause '而不知其可乐之处甚多' introduces the idea that eunuchs have many sources of joy.
  • The phrase '试为子偻指注之' suggests a detailed explanation will follow.
  • The sentence '夫王公至贵者也，然望天子之居，不翅天上' contrasts the high status of nobles with the even higher status of the emperor.
  • The clause '彼以阉故，得出入不禁，一乐也' explains one of the joys of being a eunuch.

  • 一食頃 - After one meal's time. '一食頃' is a unit of time, roughly equivalent to an hour.
  • 釶然一物，沖煙突火而遁 - Suddenly, something emerged, rushing through the smoke and fire. '釶然' describes a sudden, unexpected appearance.
  • 迅走如風，追之不及 - Ran swiftly like the wind, unable to catch up. '迅走如風' is a metaphor for extreme speed.
  • 又連出四頭，一白三黑 - Then four more emerged, one white and three black. This suggests a family group of foxes.
  • 皆不能逐 - All could not be chased. '逐' means 'to chase' or 'to pursue'.
  • 此即所謂諸女郎也 - This is what is called the 'many daughters'. '諸女郎' refers to the foxes, often personified as female spirits in Chinese folklore.
  • 與族俱行，此举徒劳矣 - They came together with their clan, so this effort was futile. '徒劳' means 'in vain' or 'futile'.
  • 欣欣然歸 - Returned happily. '欣欣然' describes a joyful and contented state.
  • 述之於董 - Told it to Dong. '述之' means 'to recount' or 'to narrate'.
  • 胡不见商，则独冒此险 - Why didn't you see the merchant, yet you alone took this risk? '胡' is a rhetorical question meaning 'why'.
  • 彼皆甘人者 - They are all willing to harm people. '甘人者' refers to creatures that are dangerous or harmful to humans.
  • 既皆逸去，必图报复 - Since they have all escaped, they will surely seek revenge. '逸去' means 'to escape', and '图报复' means 'to plan revenge'.
  • 兄不可疏防 - You must not be careless. '疏防' means 'to be careless' or 'to neglect precautions'.
  • 为兄计，不如暂归，以避其祟 - For your sake, it would be better to return temporarily to avoid their curses. '祟' refers to supernatural harm or curses.
  • 予正望其来，岂可反为葸避 - I am just waiting for them to come, how can I turn back and be timid? '葸避' means 'to be timid' or 'to avoid'.
  • 阳称其勇，而阴为护卫 - Pretended to praise his courage while secretly guarding him. '阳' means 'publicly' or 'openly', and '阴' means 'secretly' or 'in secret'.
